name: Beef Puffery
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound phyllo or strudel pastry dough, 12 sheets
3/4 pound sausage, partially thawed, if frozen
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove phyllo from refrigerator 2 hours before using. Do not remove from package, as it dries out quickly.
2. Brown sausage in a large skillet for 10 minutes, breaking up pieces with a wooden spoon. Stir in ground round and cook for 5 minutes more until meat loses its pink color. Add onions and garlic; cook until soft.
3. Tilt the skillet and remove all fat (at least 1/2 cup). Return the skillet to heat; stir in parsley, basil, oregano, salt, pepper, and tomato paste; cook for 5 minutes.
4. Remove the meat mixture to a large bowl; cool; place in refrigerator until ready to use.
5. Beat egg in a medium-sized bowl until foamy; stir in ricotta and Parmesan cheeses; chill until ready to use.
6. Combine chilled cheese mixture and chilled meat. Form into a roll 9-inch long and 2 inches high on foil; refrigerate while preparing phyllo.
7. Unfold phyllo on wax paper; remove one sheet to another piece of wax paper; cover remaining sheets with a damp towel; brush the single sheet with melted butter. Cover with second sheet of pastry; brush with butter. Repeat, using 12 sheets of phyllo and brushing each with butter. Place meat roll along short edge; roll up, jelly roll style, tucking in ends and brushing with butter after each turn. Place a piece of foil over the ends of rolls; secure with toothpicks so filling will not come out. Place seam-side down on a lightly oiled 15 X 10-inch jelly roll pan. Refrigerate until time to bake.
(Note: Can be prepared ahead up to this point.)
8. Bake in a preheated 400°F degree oven for 35 minutes or until golden. Remove the pan with meat to wire rack. Let stand about 30 minutes, then remove to serving tray with a wide spatula. Serve warm but not hot.
